  2049→        exchange_wechat: Click "交换微信" in IM tab after sending
  2050→        chat_btn_handle: Pre-located "立即沟通" button element handle (avoids clicking wrong card)
  2051→
  2052→    Returns:
  2053→        True if message was sent successfully
  2054→    """
  2055→    if config is None:
  2056→        config = get_config()
  2057→
  2058→    try:
  2059→        # Click 立即沟通 (use pre-located handle if available to avoid wrong card)
  2060→        if chat_btn_handle:
  2061→            await chat_btn_handle.click()
  2062→        else:
  2063→            chat_sel = config.get_selector("talent_bank.chat_now_button")
  2064→            chat_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(chat_sel, timeout=5000)
  2065→            if not chat_btn:
  2066→                logger.warning("Talent bank: 立即沟通 button not found")
  2067→                return False
  2068→            await human_click(page, chat_sel)
  2069→        await async_random_delay("action")
  2070→
  2071→        # Try to fill custom message in textarea (if dialog has one)
  2072→        textarea_sel = config.get_selector("talent_bank.message_textarea")
  2073→        try:
  2074→            textarea = await page.wait_for_selector(textarea_sel, timeout=3000)
  2075→            if textarea:
  2076→                # Force-set value via native setter to bypass React controlled state
  2077→                # (platform may pre-fill candidate name; fill() can merge instead of replace)
  2078→                await textarea.evaluate('''(el, text) => {
  2079→                    const setter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(
  2080→                        HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype, 'value'
  2081→                    ).set;
  2082→                    setter.call(el, '');
  2083→                    el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));
  2084→                    setter.call(el, text);
  2085→                    el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));
  2086→                    el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));
  2087→                }''', content)
  2088→                await async_random_delay("action")
  2089→        except Exception:
  2090→            logger.debug("Talent bank: no message textarea found, platform default greeting will be used")
  2091→
  2092→        # Click 发送后继续沟通
  2093→        send_sel = config.get_selector("talent_bank.send_continue_button")
  2094→        send_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(send_sel, timeout=5000)
  2095→        if not send_btn:
  2096→            logger.warning("Talent bank: send button not found")
  2097→            return False
  2098→
  2099→        await human_click(page, send_sel)
  2100→        await async_random_delay("action")
  2101→
  2102→        # Wait for IM tab to open (after clicking "发送后继续沟通")
  2103→        im_open_ms = config.get("timings.im_tab_open_wait_ms", 3000)
  2104→        await page.wait_for_timeout(im_open_ms)
  2105→
  2106→        # If expecting exchange but no new tab yet, wait a bit longer
  2107→        if (exchange_phone or exchange_wechat) and len(browser.context.pages) <= 1:
  2108→            logger.debug("Talent bank: no new tab yet, waiting extra 3s for IM tab...")
  2109→            await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
  2110→
  2111→        # --- Exchange phone/WeChat in the IM tab ---
  2112→        if (exchange_phone or exchange_wechat) and len(browser.context.pages) > 1:
  2113→            await _exchange_in_im_tab(browser, config, exchange_phone, exchange_wechat)
  2114→
  2115→        # Close IM tabs
  2116→        await _close_im_tabs(browser, page)
  2117→
  2118→        # Close any modal/popup on search page
  2119→        close_modal_sel = config.get_selector("talent_bank.close_modal")
  2120→        try:
  2121→            close_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(close_modal_sel, timeout=2000)
  2122→            if close_btn:
  2123→                await close_btn.click()
  2124→                await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
  2125→        except Exception as e:
  2126→            logger.debug(f"Talent bank: close modal best-effort failed: {e}")
  2127→
  2128→        logger.info("Talent bank: message sent inline")
  2129→        return True
  2130→
  2131→    except Exception as e:
  2132→        logger.error(f"Talent bank inline send failed: {e}")
  2133→        return False

   923→                        sent = True

   931→                    break
   932→                except QuotaExhaustedError as e:
   933→                    # Quota is a global resource — abort the entire batch
   934→                    remaining = len(send_list) - (results['sent'] + results['failed'])
   935→                    results['failed'] += 1
   936→                    results['details'].append({
   937→                        'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],

   957→                    })
   958→                    console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
   959→                    rate_limit_hit = True

   961→                except Exception as e:
   962→                    last_error = str(e)
   963→
   964→                # Retry with exponential backoff if not last attempt
   965→                if attempt < max_retries and not rate_limit_hit:
   966→                    from src.utils.delays import get_retry_delay
   967→                    delay = get_retry_delay(attempt, base_delay=3.0, max_delay=30.0)
   968→                    logger.info(f"Retry {attempt + 1}/{max_retries} for {c['name']} in {delay:.1f}s")
   969→                    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   970→
   971→            if not sent and not rate_limit_hit:
   972→                actual_attempts = attempt + 1
   973→                results['failed'] += 1
   974→                results['details'].append({
   975→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],

   990→
   991→            # Human-like delay between messages

   994→    return results

   997→def render_template(template_content: str, variables: Dict[str, str], validate: bool = True) -> str:
   998→    """
   999→    Render template with variables.
  1000→
  1001→    Maimai chat is plain text, so values are substituted as-is without
  1002→    HTML escaping (which would produce visible & entities in chat).
  1003→
  1004→    Args:
  1005→        template_content: Template string with {variable} placeholders
  1006→        variables: Variable values to substitute
  1007→        validate: Whether to validate template before rendering (default True)
  1008→
  1009→    Returns:
  1010→        Rendered message content
  1011→
  1012→    Raises:
  1013→        ValidationError: If template validation fails and validate=True
  1014→    """
  1015→    if validate:
  1016→        # Validate template can be fully rendered
  1017→        is_valid, missing = validate_template_rendering(template_content, variables, {})
  1018→        if not is_valid:
  1019→            raise ValidationError(
  1020→                f"模板变量缺失或为空: {', '.join(missing)}\n"
  1021→                f"请检查模板或提供完整变量"
  1022→            )
  1023→
  1024→    content = template_content
  1025→    for key, value in variables.items():
  1026→        content = content.replace(f"{{{key}}}", str(value))
  1027→    return content
  1028→
  1029→
  1030→def preview_message(
  1031→    template_name: str,
  1032→    variables: Dict[str, str],
  1033→    candidate_info: Optional[Dict] = None
  1034→) -> str:
  1035→    """
  1036→    Preview a message without sending.
  1037→
  1038→    Args:
  1039→        template_name: Name of template
  1040→        variables: Variables for template
  1041→        candidate_info: Optional candidate info for context
  1042→
  1043→    Returns:
  1044→        Rendered message content
  1045→    """
  1046→    config = get_config()
  1047→    template = config.get_template(template_name)
  1048→    content = render_template(template['content'], variables)
  1049→
  1050→    # Display preview
  1051→    display_message_preview(content, candidate_info, template_name)
  1052→
  1053→    return content
  1054→
  1055→
  1056→def display_message_preview(
  1057→    content: str,
  1058→    candidate_info: Optional[Dict] = None,
  1059→    template_name: Optional[str] = None
  1060→) -> None:
  1061→    """
  1062→    Display formatted message preview in terminal.
  1063→
  1064→    Args:
  1065→        content: Message content
  1066→        candidate_info: Optional candidate info
  1067→        template_name: Optional template name
  1068→    """
  1069→    # Build recipient line
  1070→    if candidate_info:
  1071→        recipient = f"{candidate_info.get('name', 'Unknown')}"
  1072→        if candidate_info.get('title') and candidate_info.get('company'):
  1073→            recipient += f" ({candidate_info['title']} @ {candidate_info['company']})"
  1074→    else:
  1075→        recipient = "Unknown recipient"
  1076→
  1077→    # Create panel
  1078→    title = "Message Preview"
  1079→    if template_name:
  1080→        title += f" [{template_name}]"
  1081→
  1082→    panel_content = Text()

62→async def send_message_batch(
    63→    req: MessageBatchRequest,
    64→    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    65→    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
    66→):
    67→    """Batch send messages to multiple candidates (queued)."""
    68→
    69→    async def _run():
    70→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    71→        from src.modules.message import send_message_batch as _batch_send
    72→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    73→        from src.models.database import Candidate, get_db_manager
    74→
    75→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    76→        await browser.start()
    77→        await ensure_logged_in(browser)
    78→
    79→        # Look up candidate info from DB (batch query)
    80→        db = get_db_manager()
    81→        candidates = []
    82→        with db.session_scope() as session:
    83→            rows = (
    84→                session.query(Candidate)
    85→                .filter(Candidate.maimai_id.in_(req.maimai_ids))
    86→                .all()
    87→            )
    88→            db_map = {c.maimai_id: c for c in rows}
    89→
    90→            for mid in req.maimai_ids:
    91→                c = db_map.get(mid)
    92→                if c:
    93→                    candidates.append({
    94→                        "maimai_id": c.maimai_id,
    95→                        "name": c.name or "Unknown",
    96→                        "company": c.company or "",
    97→                        "title": c.title or "",
    98→                        "profile_url": c.profile_url or "",
    99→                    })
   100→                else:
   101→                    candidates.append({
   102→                        "maimai_id": mid,
   103→                        "name": "Unknown",
   104→                        "company": "",
   105→                        "title": "",
   106→                    })
   107→
   108→        return await _batch_send(
   109→            candidates=candidates,
   110→            template_name=req.template,
   111→            common_variables=req.variables,
   112→            browser=browser,
   113→            skip_confirmation=True,
   114→        )
   115→
   116→    task = BrowserTask(
   117→        name=f"message_batch:{len(req.maimai_ids)}",
   118→        coroutine_factory=_run,
   119→        priority=TaskPriority.NORMAL,
   120→    )
   121→
   122→    cb = broadcaster.make_progress_callback(task.task_id)
   123→    original_factory = task.coroutine_factory
   124→
   125→    async def _wrapped():
   126→        token = set_progress_callback(cb)
   127→        try:
   128→            return await original_factory()
   129→        finally:
   130→            reset_progress_callback(token)
   131→
   132→    task.coroutine_factory = _wrapped
   133→    await queue.submit(task)
   134→
   135→    return TaskResponse(task_id=task.task_id, status="queued", position=queue.queue_size)
   136→

1→"""Message send endpoints — single and batch."""
     2→
     3→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
     4→
     5→from src.web.deps import get_queue, get_broadcaster
     6→from src.web.schemas import MessageSendRequest, MessageBatchRequest, TaskResponse
     7→from src.web.task_queue import BrowserTask, TaskPriority, TaskQueue
     8→from src.web.progress import Broadcaster, set_progress_callback, reset_progress_callback
     9→
    10→router = APIRouter()
    11→
    12→
    13→@router.post("/message", response_model=TaskResponse)
    14→async def send_message(
    15→    req: MessageSendRequest,
    16→    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    17→    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
    18→):
    19→    """Send a single message (queued)."""
    20→
    21→    async def _run():
    22→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    23→        from src.modules.message import send_message as _send_message
    24→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    25→
    26→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    27→        await browser.start()
    28→        await ensure_logged_in(browser)

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: Batch Sending Functions Refactoring

I've completed a thorough analysis of all callers and related code for the three `send_message_batch*` functions. Here are my findings:

---

### 1. ALL CALLERS

#### **send_message_batch** (Path A: Search Center)
- **Pipeline wrapper**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/services/pipeline.py:222-257` — `async def send_batch()`

  - Line 859: `await send_batch(...)` in `run_pipeline_batched()` when mode != ENTERPRISE/TALENT_BANK
- **Web router**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/web/routers/message.py:62-135`
  - Line 62: `async def send_message_batch()` (web endpoint wrapper)
  - Line 108: calls `_batch_send()` (deferred import of `send_message_batch`)

#### **send_message_batch_enterprise** (Path B: Enterprise)
- **Pipeline wrapper**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/services/pipeline.py:260-293` — `async def send_batch_enterprise()`
  - Line 285: calls `send_message_batch_enterprise()`
- **Pipeline orchestration callers**:
  - Line 705: `await send_batch_enterprise(...)` in `run_pipeline_default()` when mode = ENTERPRISE
  - Line 847: `await send_batch_enterprise(...)` in `run_pipeline_batched()` when mode = ENTERPRISE
- **Web router**: Not directly called from web (only through send_batch wrapper which uses send_message_batch)

#### **send_message_batch_talent_bank** (Path C: Talent Bank)
- **Pipeline wrapper**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/services/pipeline.py:296-335` — `async def send_batch_talent_bank()`
  - Line 325: calls `send_message_batch_talent_bank()`
- **Pipeline orchestration callers**:
  - Line 630: `await send_batch_talent_bank(...)` in `run_pipeline_default()` (per-keyword inline send)
  - Line 852: `await send_batch_talent_bank(...)` in `run_pipeline_batched()` when mode = TALENT_BANK
- **Web router**: Not directly called from web

---

### 2. THE `_log_message` HELPER

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/message.py:1550-1583`

```python
def _log_message(
    db_manager,
    maimai_id: str,
    template_name: str,
    content: str,
    status: str
) -> None:
    """Log message to database. Creates a minimal Candidate if needed for FK."""
```

**Key behaviors**:
- Ensures candidate exists for FK constraint (creates if missing)
- Logs `Message` record with status (sent/cancelled/failed)
- Sets `sent_at` only if status='sent'
- Re-raises on audit failure for 'sent' status (critical)
- Called at these lines in batch functions:
  - Line 1879 (enterprise): after successful send
  - Line 2360 (talent_bank): after successful send
  - Line 2373 (talent_bank): after failed send

---

### 3. INLINE SEND FUNCTION SIGNATURES

#### **send_message_enterprise_inline** (Path B inline)
**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/message.py:1615-1687`

```python

    page: Page,
    content: str,
    config=None,
) -> bool:
```

**Returns**: `True` if sent successfully, `False` otherwise

**Called by**: `send_message_batch_enterprise()` at line 1864

---

#### **send_message_talent_bank_inline** (Path C inline)
**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/message.py:2027-2133`

```python
async def send_message_talent_bank_inline(
    page: Page,
    content: str,
    browser: 'BrowserManager',

    chat_btn_handle=None,
) -> bool:
```

**Returns**: `True` if sent successfully, `False` otherwise

**Called by**: `send_message_batch_talent_bank()` at line 2340-2345

---

### 4. SINGLE MESSAGE SEND (PATH A)

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/message.py:447-639` (signature + core logic)

```python
async def send_message(
    maimai_id: str,
    template_name: str,
    variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
    interactive: bool = False,
    discovered_selectors: Optional['DiscoveredSelectors'] = None,
    _skip_rate_check: bool = False,
    profile_url: Optional[str] = None,
) -> bool:
```

**Returns**: `True` if sent successfully, `False` otherwise

**Called from batch loop** at lines 900-910:
- Wrapped in `asyncio.wait_for()` with 90-second timeout per candidate
- Called with `_skip_rate_check=True` (batch loop checks rate limit before each iteration)
- Called with `skip_confirmation=True` (pre-validated in batch)
- Called with `discovered_selectors` passed from batch for interactive mode

**Retry logic in batch**: max 2 retries with exponential backoff (lines 897-969)

---

### 5. PIPELINE WRAPPERS

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/services/pipeline.py:222-335`

All three wrappers have the same pattern:

#### **send_batch** (Path A wrapper)
**Lines**: 222-257
- Input: `List[CandidateBasic]`
- Sorts by score via `sort_candidates_by_score()`
- Converts to dicts via `_candidates_to_dicts()`
- Calls `send_message_batch()`

#### **send_batch_enterprise** (Path B wrapper)
**Lines**: 260-293
- Same as send_batch but calls `send_message_batch_enterprise()`
- **Takes `page=browser.page` parameter** (differs from Path A)

#### **send_batch_talent_bank** (Path C wrapper)
**Lines**: 296-335
- Same as send_batch but calls `send_message_batch_talent_bank()`
- Adds `exchange_phone=exchange_phone` and `exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat` parameters
- **Takes `page=browser.page` parameter** (differs from Path A)

---

### 6. BATCH FUNCTION IMPLEMENTATIONS

#### **send_message_batch** (Path A)
**Lines**: 683-994

**Signature**:
```python
async def send_message_batch(
    candidates: List[dict],
    template_name: str,
    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: BrowserManager,
    skip_messaged: bool = True,
    interactive: bool = False,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
```

**Flow**:
1. Load template & validate
2. Create `DiscoveredSelectors()` if interactive
3. Filter already-messaged candidates (N+1 prevention via batch query)
4. Pre-validate all candidates can render template
5. Build message preview for first candidate
6. Display summary panel + single confirmation
7. Loop through candidates with progress bar:
   - Check rate limit (waits min-interval)
   - Build per-candidate variables (name, company, position)
   - Render template
   - Call `send_message()` with retries (max 2, exponential backoff)
   - Log via `_log_message()`
   - Handle QuotaExhaustedError, RateLimitExceededError, PermanentFailureError
   - Record results
   - Emit progress via `emit_progress()`
   - Random delay between messages

**Returns**: `Dict[str, Any]` with:
- `total`, `sent`, `failed`, `skipped`, `rate_limited` counts
- `details`: list of per-candidate result dicts

---

#### **send_message_batch_enterprise** (Path B)
**Lines**: 1690-1900

**Signature**:
```python
async def send_message_batch_enterprise(
    page: Page,
    candidates: List[dict],
    template_name: str,
    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: 'BrowserManager',
    skip_messaged: bool = True,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
```

**Key differences from Path A**:
- NO `interactive` parameter (no selector discovery for inline mode)
- NO retry loop (one-shot send via `send_message_enterprise_inline()`)
- NO `_skip_rate_check` flag (rate limit checked per candidate)
- Scrolls to candidate card by name match (line 1857-1859)
- Calls `send_message_enterprise_inline(page, content, config)` (line 1864)
- No timeout wrapper around inline send

**Otherwise identical to Path A**:
- Same filtering/validation logic
- Same preview & confirmation
- Same progress bar
- Same result tracking
- Same delay between messages

---

#### **send_message_batch_talent_bank** (Path C)
**Lines**: 2136-2378

**Signature**:
```python
async def send_message_batch_talent_bank(
    page: Page,
    candidates: List[dict],
    template_name: str,
    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: 'BrowserManager',
    skip_messaged: bool = True,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
    exchange_phone: bool = False,
    exchange_wechat: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
```

**Key differences from Path B**:
- Adds `exchange_phone` & `exchange_wechat` parameters
- More complex card-button location (lines 2304-2337):
  - Uses `page.evaluate_handle()` to traverse DOM from name element
  - Passes `chat_btn_handle` to inline send (avoids wrong card click)
- Calls `send_message_talent_bank_inline()` with exchange flags

**Otherwise identical to Path B**:
- No retry loop, no interactive mode
- Same filtering/validation/preview/confirmation
- Same progress bar and result tracking

---

### 7. WEB ROUTER THAT CALLS BATCH FUNCTIONS

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/web/routers/message.py:1-136`

#### **POST /message/batch**
**Lines**: 61-135

```python
@router.post("/message/batch", response_model=TaskResponse)
async def send_message_batch(
    req: MessageBatchRequest,
    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
):
```

**Behavior**:
- Accepts `MessageBatchRequest` with `maimai_ids`, `template`, `variables`
- Queues task with progress callback
- Inside task:
  - Starts browser & ensures login
  - Batch-queries Candidate table to get name/company/title
  - Falls back to minimal dicts if not in DB
  - Calls `send_message_batch()` from modules
  - Progress callback emits updates

**Important**: The web router ONLY uses `send_message_batch` (Path A), not the enterprise or talent_bank versions. Enterprise/talent_bank batch sending is only exposed via CLI (`src/cli/main.py pipeline`).

---

### 8. TESTS

**Current status**: **NO tests for batch sending**

- `tests/test_config.py` and `tests/test_validators.py` exist but don't cover batch functions
- No integration tests for any `send_message_batch*` variants
- Browser operations (inline sends) are difficult to mock without full Playwright setup

---

## SUMMARY OF STRUCTURAL DUPLICATION

All three batch functions (~300 lines each) duplicate:

| Aspect | Lines | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Filter already-messaged | ~30 | **Identical** N+1 prevention pattern |
| Template validation | ~20 | **Identical** pre-validation loops |
| Preview & confirmation | ~60 | **Identical** Rich panel + questionary |
| Progress bar setup | ~5 | **Identical** |
| Main loop structure | ~100 | **Highly similar** (varies only in send call) |
| Rate limit handling | ~30 | **Very similar** (Path A has retry, others don't) |
| Result tracking | ~30 | **Identical** detail dict recording |
| Progress emission | ~5 | **Identical** `emit_progress()` calls |
| Random delays | ~2 | **Identical** `async_random_delay()` |

**Refactoring opportunity**: Extract a generic orchestration function that takes a callback for the actual send operation (either `send_message`, `send_message_enterprise_inline`, or `send_message_talent_bank_inline`).

---

This analysis is complete and ready for your refactoring work!

- Sorts by score via `sort_candidates_by_score()`
- Converts to dicts via `_candidates_to_dicts()`
- Calls `send_message_batch()`

#### **send_batch_enterprise** (Path B wrapper)
**Lines**: 260-293
- Same as send_batch but calls `send_message_batch_enterprise()`
- **Takes `page=browser.page` parameter** (differs from Path A)

#### **send_batch_talent_bank** (Path C wrapper)
**Lines**: 296-335
- Same as send_batch but calls `send_message_batch_talent_bank()`
- Adds `exchange_phone=exchange_phone` and `exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat` parameters
- **Takes `page=browser.page` parameter** (differs from Path A)

---

### 6. BATCH FUNCTION IMPLEMENTATIONS

#### **send_message_batch** (Path A)
**Lines**: 683-994

**Signature**:
```python
async def send_message_batch(
    candidates: List[dict],
    template_name: str,
    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: BrowserManager,
    skip_messaged: bool = True,
    interactive: bool = False,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
```

**Flow**:
1. Load template & validate
2. Create `DiscoveredSelectors()` if interactive
3. Filter already-messaged candidates (N+1 prevention via batch query)
4. Pre-validate all candidates can render template
5. Build message preview for first candidate
6. Display summary panel + single confirmation
7. Loop through candidates with progress bar:
   - Check rate limit (waits min-interval)
   - Build per-candidate variables (name, company, position)
   - Render template
   - Call `send_message()` with retries (max 2, exponential backoff)
   - Log via `_log_message()`
   - Handle QuotaExhaustedError, RateLimitExceededError, PermanentFailureError
   - Record results
   - Emit progress via `emit_progress()`
   - Random delay between messages

**Returns**: `Dict[str, Any]` with:
- `total`, `sent`, `failed`, `skipped`, `rate_limited` counts
- `details`: list of per-candidate result dicts

---

#### **send_message_batch_enterprise** (Path B)
**Lines**: 1690-1900

**Signature**:
```python
async def send_message_batch_enterprise(
    page: Page,
    candidates: List[dict],
    template_name: str,
    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: 'BrowserManager',
    skip_messaged: bool = True,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
```

**Key differences from Path A**:
- NO `interactive` parameter (no selector discovery for inline mode)
- NO retry loop (one-shot send via `send_message_enterprise_inline()`)
- NO `_skip_rate_check` flag (rate limit checked per candidate)
- Scrolls to candidate card by name match (line 1857-1859)
- Calls `send_message_enterprise_inline(page, content, config)` (line 1864)
- No timeout wrapper around inline send

**Otherwise identical to Path A**:
- Same filtering/validation logic
- Same preview & confirmation
- Same progress bar
- Same result tracking
- Same delay between messages

---

#### **send_message_batch_talent_bank** (Path C)
**Lines**: 2136-2378

**Signature**:
```python
async def send_message_batch_talent_bank(
    page: Page,
    candidates: List[dict],
    template_name: str,
    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
    browser: 'BrowserManager',
    skip_messaged: bool = True,
    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
    exchange_phone: bool = False,
    exchange_wechat: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
```

**Key differences from Path B**:
- Adds `exchange_phone` & `exchange_wechat` parameters
- More complex card-button location (lines 2304-2337):
  - Uses `page.evaluate_handle()` to traverse DOM from name element
  - Passes `chat_btn_handle` to inline send (avoids wrong card click)
- Calls `send_message_talent_bank_inline()` with exchange flags

**Otherwise identical to Path B**:
- No retry loop, no interactive mode
- Same filtering/validation/preview/confirmation
- Same progress bar and result tracking

---

### 7. WEB ROUTER THAT CALLS BATCH FUNCTIONS

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/web/routers/message.py:1-136`

#### **POST /message/batch**
**Lines**: 61-135

```python
@router.post("/message/batch", response_model=TaskResponse)
async def send_message_batch(
    req: MessageBatchRequest,
    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
):
```

**Behavior**:
- Accepts `MessageBatchRequest` with `maimai_ids`, `template`, `variables`
- Queues task with progress callback
- Inside task:
  - Starts browser & ensures login
  - Batch-queries Candidate table to get name/company/title
  - Falls back to minimal dicts if not in DB
  - Calls `send_message_batch()` from modules
  - Progress callback emits updates

**Important**: The web router ONLY uses `send_message_batch` (Path A), not the enterprise or talent_bank versions. Enterprise/talent_bank batch sending is only exposed via CLI (`src/cli/main.py pipeline`).

---

### 8. TESTS

**Current status**: **NO tests for batch sending**

- `tests/test_config.py` and `tests/test_validators.py` exist but don't cover batch functions
- No integration tests for any `send_message_batch*` variants
- Browser operations (inline sends) are difficult to mock without full Playwright setup

---

## SUMMARY OF STRUCTURAL DUPLICATION

All three batch functions (~300 lines each) duplicate:

| Aspect | Lines | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Filter already-messaged | ~30 | **Identical** N+1 prevention pattern |
| Template validation | ~20 | **Identical** pre-validation loops |
| Preview & confirmation | ~60 | **Identical** Rich panel + questionary |
| Progress bar setup | ~5 | **Identical** |
| Main loop structure | ~100 | **Highly similar** (varies only in send call) |
| Rate limit handling | ~30 | **Very similar** (Path A has retry, others don't) |
| Result tracking | ~30 | **Identical** detail dict recording |
| Progress emission | ~5 | **Identical** `emit_progress()` calls |
| Random delays | ~2 | **Identical** `async_random_delay()` |

**Refactoring opportunity**: Extract a generic orchestration function that takes a callback for the actual send operation (either `send_message`, `send_message_enterprise_inline`, or `send_message_talent_bank_inline`).

---

This analysis is complete and ready for your refactoring work!

Good, I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me now design the plan. Let me think about the approach:

The key insight is that all three functions follow the same orchestration:
1. Init (config, rate_limiter, db_manager, template)
2. Filter already-messaged
3. Pre-validate (Path A only, but could be a no-op for B/C)
4. Preview + confirm
5. Loop with progress bar:
   - Rate limit check
   - Build variables + render template
   - **SEND** (this is the only varying part)
   - Record result
   - Emit progress
   - Delay
   
So I'll extract a common orchestration function that accepts a `send_one` callback, where each path provides its own implementation—Path A needs retry logic with timeouts and selector handling, Path B handles enterprise-specific delivery, and Path C manages the Slack integration.

But I'm noticing the result handling diverges too: Path A tracks attempt counts and catches quota exhaustion to break the outer loop, while B and C just record simple success/failure outcomes.

I think the callback should return something richer that captures all the different outcomes—sent, failed, or abort when we hit quota or rate limits. Let me define a cleaner result type that the orchestrator can use to decide whether to keep processing or stop.

Now I'm looking at how the actual retry loops work across the different paths to understand what information needs to flow back from the callback. I'm noticing Path A is the only one emitting progress updates in the loop, which might be an oversight in the other paths. For the refactoring, I'll define a callback protocol that each mode implements with its own closure state, and have the orchestrator handle the common concerns like rate limiting, variable rendering, and progress tracking. The callback returns a result object that signals success, failure reasons, and whether to abort the batch or just this message.

The key differences between paths are how they interact with the DOM and log to the database—Path A does it internally in `send_message()`, while B and C handle it in their callbacks. I can also make pre-validation optional since only Path A does it upfront, and the `interactive` parameter is only used in Path A, so I'll need to account for that variation too.

Now looking at the pipeline wrappers, they're all doing similar work—sorting candidates and converting to dicts before dispatching to the message layer. I could consolidate these into a single wrapper that handles the dispatch internally, but that's probably a bigger refactor than needed right now. For now I'll keep the focus on cleaning up message.py itself.

But I should also consider the web router, which only uses the search_center path. After refactoring, it'll need to call the unified function with the appropriate mode or callback parameter, and I need to make sure the public API stays backward compatible. Let me think through what the API should look like—either a single function with a mode parameter or keeping separate functions as thin wrappers.

Defining the wrapper approach...

I'm leaning toward Option 2 with thin public wrappers that delegate to a shared internal function, keeping the external API stable so pipeline.py and the web router don't need changes. The real benefit is consolidating all the batch-sending logic in one place while maintaining backward compatibility, and potentially we could later simplify pipeline.py by merging those three wrapper calls into a single entry point.

Now I need to think through how the `SendOneResult` dataclass should handle the different outcomes the orchestrator needs to track. The callback can produce sent, failed, or abort scenarios (quota/rate limit), and the retry path has its own set of possible results—success after retries, permanent failures, quota exhaustion, rate limits, or timeouts—while the simpler paths just return success.

The dataclass needs to capture the status, the reason it ended up that way, how many attempts were made, and the content. Then the orchestrator checks the status and updates the results counters and details accordingly, breaking the loop if it hits a quota or rate limit abort.

Defining the dataclass and orchestrator logic...

Actually, I'm overcomplicating this. Instead of having the orchestrator handle all these status checks, I can have the send function raise specific exceptions for abort scenarios—like QuotaExhaustedError or RateLimitExceededError—and let the orchestrator catch those exceptions rather than checking status strings.

But wait, that couples the orchestrator to those specific exceptions, which isn't ideal either. Let me stick with the dataclass approach since it's cleaner and keeps things decoupled. I'll define a SendOneResult type that includes fields for whether to abort the batch and how to count remaining items, then write out the orchestrator logic that uses this structure.

The orchestrator will handle initialization, filter out already-messaged candidates, return early if there's nothing left, optionally pre-validate templates, show a preview and get confirmation, then loop through with a progress bar where it checks rate limits, renders the template, calls the send_one function, and records results based on the returned status.

Now I'm wrapping this common logic with thin public functions that create the appropriate sender based on the context—one for the standard pipeline using a search center sender, and another for enterprise mode with a different sender implementation. Writing sender factory functions... Finishing up the retry logic with a final result that includes the attempt count, then moving on to think about consolidating the pipeline.py file where I notice three nearly identical wrapper functions that could probably be unified.

Refactoring these batch sending functions into a single parameterized version...

I can consolidate the mode-specific logic by checking the search mode and routing to the appropriate backend function, then simplifying all the callers to use just one function instead of multiple if/elif branches.

Now I'm mapping out the refactoring steps: I'll add a dataclass for the result type, create a shared orchestrator function that handles the common logic, build factory functions for each sender variant, update the three public functions to use the orchestrator, merge the pipeline wrappers into a single entry point, and update the callers to use the simplified interface.

I need to design a refactoring plan for consolidating three nearly-identical `send_message_batch*` functions in a Python project. Here's the full context:

## Current State

File: `src/modules/message.py` (~2400 lines)

Three batch functions with ~70% identical code:
1. `send_message_batch()` (line 683-994, ~310 lines) — Path A: search_center
2. `send_message_batch_enterprise()` (line 1690-1900, ~210 lines) — Path B: enterprise  
3. `send_message_batch_talent_bank()` (line 2136-2378, ~240 lines) — Path C: talent_bank

### Identical parts across all three (~70%):
- Init: load config/rate_limiter/db_manager/template
- Filter already-messaged: batch pre-fetch sent IDs from DB
- Empty list check
- Preview panel: Rich Text summary + first candidate preview (only mode label differs)
- Confirmation: questionary.confirm or skip_confirmation
- Progress bar: Rich Progress
- Per-candidate loop structure: rate limit check → build variables → render template → [SEND] → record result → progress.advance → delay
- Result dict tracking: total/sent/failed/skipped/rate_limited/details

### Different parts — only the per-candidate send logic:

**Path A (search_center):**
- Pre-validates all templates upfront (B/C don't)
- Has retry loop (max_retries=2, exponential backoff)
- Calls `send_message(maimai_id, template_name, variables, browser, skip_confirmation=True, interactive, discovered_selectors, _skip_rate_check=True, profile_url)` wrapped in `asyncio.wait_for(timeout=90)`
- Handles QuotaExhaustedError (abort batch), PermanentFailureError (skip), TimeoutError (skip)
- send_message() internally logs to DB
- Records `attempts` in detail dict
- Emits `emit_progress("send_progress", ...)` (B/C don't)
- Has `interactive` + `discovered_selectors` for selector discovery

**Path B (enterprise):**
- Scrolls to candidate card by name
- Calls `send_message_enterprise_inline(page, content, config) -> bool`
- Calls `rate_limiter.log_action()` and `_log_message()` explicitly
- No retry, no timeout

**Path C (talent_bank):**
- Scrolls to card + DOM traverse to find "立即沟通" button handle via `page.evaluate_handle()`
- Calls `send_message_talent_bank_inline(page, content, browser, config, exchange_phone, exchange_wechat, chat_btn_handle) -> bool`
- Calls `rate_limiter.log_action()` and `_log_message()` explicitly  
- No retry, no timeout
- Has `exchange_phone`/`exchange_wechat` params

## Callers

In `src/services/pipeline.py`:
- Three thin wrappers: `send_batch()`, `send_batch_enterprise()`, `send_batch_talent_bank()` — all sort by score, convert to dicts, then call the corresponding batch function
- Called from `run_pipeline_default()` and `run_pipeline_batched()` with mode-based if/elif dispatch

In `src/web/routers/message.py`:
- Only calls `send_message_batch()` (Path A)

## Design Requirements
1. Keep the three public function signatures unchanged (callers don't need to change)
2. Extract common orchestration into a private `_send_message_batch_common()` 
3. Use callback/closure pattern for per-candidate send logic
4. Optionally merge the three pipeline.py wrappers into one `send_batch(mode=...)`
5. No new dependencies
6. Python 3.9+ compatible

Please provide:
1. The recommended architecture (dataclass for callback result, common function signature, callback factory pattern)
2. Detailed step-by-step implementation plan
3. Risks and edge cases to watch out for
4. How to verify the refactoring is correct

    43→

    45→def _extract_user_id(profile_url: str) -> Optional[str]:
    46→    """Extract dstu/user_id from a Maimai profile URL for direct chat navigation.
    47→
    48→    Only returns IDs that are known to work with the chat URL (u2= parameter).
    49→    Query-param IDs (dstu, u2, egid) are reliable for chat navigation.
    50→    Path-based IDs (/contact/detail/xxx) are NOT the same as chat user IDs
    51→    and should not be used for direct chat URL construction.
    52→    """
    53→    from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs
    54→    parsed = urlparse(profile_url)
    55→    params = parse_qs(parsed.query)
    56→    # Only use query parameter IDs — these are confirmed to work with uh_chat_pc
    57→    for key in ('dstu', 'u2', 'egid'):

    38→def _auto_backup_database() -> None:
    39→    """自动备份数据库（Pipeline 完成后调用）"""
    40→    try:
    41→        import sys
    42→        from pathlib import Path
    43→        # Import backup function
    44→        sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent))
    45→        from scripts.backup_data import backup_database
    46→
    47→        success, backup_path = backup_database(silent=True)
    48→        if success:
    49→            console.print(f"[dim]数据库已自动备份: {Path(backup_path).name}[/dim]")
    50→    except Exception as e:
    51→        # Backup failure should not crash pipeline
    52→        from loguru import logger
    53→        logger.warning(f"Auto backup failed: {e}")

1→"""Message send endpoints — single and batch."""
     2→
     3→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
     4→
     5→from src.web.deps import get_queue, get_broadcaster
     6→from src.web.schemas import MessageSendRequest, MessageBatchRequest, TaskResponse
     7→from src.web.task_queue import BrowserTask, TaskPriority, TaskQueue
     8→from src.web.progress import Broadcaster, set_progress_callback, reset_progress_callback
     9→
    10→router = APIRouter()
    11→
    12→
    13→@router.post("/message", response_model=TaskResponse)
    14→async def send_message(
    15→    req: MessageSendRequest,
    16→    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    17→    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
    18→):
    19→    """Send a single message (queued)."""
    20→
    21→    async def _run():
    22→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    23→        from src.modules.message import send_message as _send_message
    24→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    25→
    26→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    27→        await browser.start()
    28→        await ensure_logged_in(browser)
    29→
    30→        success = await _send_message(
    31→            maimai_id=req.maimai_id,
    32→            template_name=req.template,
    33→            variables=req.variables,
    34→            browser=browser,
    35→            skip_confirmation=True,
    36→        )
    37→        return {"success": success, "maimai_id": req.maimai_id}
    38→
    39→    task = BrowserTask(
    40→        name=f"message:{req.maimai_id}",
    41→        coroutine_factory=_run,
    42→        priority=TaskPriority.NORMAL,
    43→    )
    44→
    45→    cb = broadcaster.make_progress_callback(task.task_id)
    46→    original_factory = task.coroutine_factory
    47→
    48→    async def _wrapped():
    49→        token = set_progress_callback(cb)
    50→        try:
    51→            return await original_factory()
    52→        finally:
    53→            reset_progress_callback(token)
    54→
    55→    task.coroutine_factory = _wrapped
    56→    await queue.submit(task)
    57→
    58→    return TaskResponse(task_id=task.task_id, status="queued", position=queue.queue_size)
    59→
    60→
    61→@router.post("/message/batch", response_model=TaskResponse)
    62→async def send_message_batch(
    63→    req: MessageBatchRequest,
    64→    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    65→    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
    66→):
    67→    """Batch send messages to multiple candidates (queued)."""
    68→
    69→    async def _run():
    70→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    71→        from src.modules.message import send_message_batch as _batch_send
    72→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    73→        from src.models.database import Candidate, get_db_manager
    74→
    75→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    76→        await browser.start()
    77→        await ensure_logged_in(browser)
    78→
    79→        # Look up candidate info from DB (batch query)
    80→        db = get_db_manager()
    81→        candidates = []
    82→        with db.session_scope() as session:
    83→            rows = (
    84→                session.query(Candidate)
    85→                .filter(Candidate.maimai_id.in_(req.maimai_ids))
    86→                .all()
    87→            )
    88→            db_map = {c.maimai_id: c for c in rows}
    89→
    90→            for mid in req.maimai_ids:
    91→                c = db_map.get(mid)
    92→                if c:
    93→                    candidates.append({
    94→                        "maimai_id": c.maimai_id,
    95→                        "name": c.name or "Unknown",
    96→                        "company": c.company or "",
    97→                        "title": c.title or "",
    98→                        "profile_url": c.profile_url or "",
    99→                    })
   100→                else:
   101→                    candidates.append({
   102→                        "maimai_id": mid,
   103→                        "name": "Unknown",
   104→                        "company": "",
   105→                        "title": "",
   106→                    })
   107→
   108→        return await _batch_send(
   109→            candidates=candidates,
   110→            template_name=req.template,
   111→            common_variables=req.variables,
   112→            browser=browser,
   113→            skip_confirmation=True,
   114→        )
   115→
   116→    task = BrowserTask(
   117→        name=f"message_batch:{len(req.maimai_ids)}",
   118→        coroutine_factory=_run,
   119→        priority=TaskPriority.NORMAL,
   120→    )
   121→
   122→    cb = broadcaster.make_progress_callback(task.task_id)
   123→    original_factory = task.coroutine_factory
   124→
   125→    async def _wrapped():
   126→        token = set_progress_callback(cb)
   127→        try:
   128→            return await original_factory()
   129→        finally:
   130→            reset_progress_callback(token)
   131→
   132→    task.coroutine_factory = _wrapped
   133→    await queue.submit(task)
   134→
   135→    return TaskResponse(task_id=task.task_id, status="queued", position=queue.queue_size)
   136→

1690→async def send_message_batch_enterprise(
  1691→    page: Page,
  1692→    candidates: List[dict],
  1693→    template_name: str,
  1694→    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
  1695→    browser: 'BrowserManager',
  1696→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
  1697→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
  1698→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  1699→    """Batch send messages inline on the enterprise recruitment page.
  1700→
  1701→    Iterates visible candidate cards on the enterprise results page,
  1702→    sending messages inline without navigating to separate chat pages.
  1703→
  1704→    Args:
  1705→        page: Playwright page on the enterprise results page
  1706→        candidates: List of candidate dicts with maimai_id, name, company, title
  1707→        template_name: Template name to use
  1708→        common_variables: Shared template variables
  1709→        browser: Browser manager instance
  1710→        skip_messaged: Skip already-messaged candidates
  1711→
  1712→    Returns:
  1713→        Dict with total, sent, failed, skipped, rate_limited counts
  1714→    """
  1715→    config = get_config()
  1716→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
  1717→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
  1718→
  1719→    try:
  1720→        template = config.get_template(template_name)
  1721→    except Exception:
  1722→        raise ValidationError(f"Template not found: {template_name}")
  1723→
  1724→    results = {
  1725→        "total": len(candidates),
  1726→        "sent": 0,
  1727→        "failed": 0,
  1728→        "skipped": 0,
  1729→        "rate_limited": 0,
  1730→        "details": [],
  1731→    }
  1732→
  1733→    # Filter already-messaged
  1734→    send_list = []
  1735→    if skip_messaged:
  1736→        # Batch pre-fetch all sent message IDs to avoid N+1 queries
  1737→        all_ids = [c["maimai_id"] for c in candidates]
  1738→        messaged_ids = set()
  1739→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
  1740→            rows = session.query(Message.candidate_maimai_id).filter(
  1741→                Message.candidate_maimai_id.in_(all_ids),
  1742→                Message.status == "sent"
  1743→            ).distinct().all()
  1744→            messaged_ids = {row[0] for row in rows}
  1745→
  1746→        for c in candidates:
  1747→            if c["maimai_id"] in messaged_ids:
  1748→                results["skipped"] += 1
  1749→                results["details"].append({
  1750→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1751→                    "name": c["name"],
  1752→                    "status": "skipped",
  1753→                    "reason": "already messaged",
  1754→                })
  1755→            else:
  1756→                send_list.append(c)
  1757→    else:
  1758→        send_list = list(candidates)
  1759→
  1760→    if not send_list:
  1761→        if results["skipped"] > 0:
  1762→            console.print(f"[yellow]All {results['skipped']} candidates already messaged. Skipping send.[/yellow]")
  1763→        else:
  1764→            console.print("[yellow]No candidates to send messages to.[/yellow]")
  1765→        return results
  1766→
  1767→    # Build preview
  1768→    first = send_list[0]
  1769→    first_vars = {
  1770→        **common_variables,
  1771→        "name": first["name"],
  1772→        "company": first["company"],
  1773→        "position": first["title"],
  1774→    }
  1775→    first_content = render_template(template["content"], first_vars)
  1776→
  1777→    # Display summary
  1778→    summary_text = Text()
  1779→    summary_text.append("Template: ", style="bold")
  1780→    summary_text.append(f"{template_name}\n")
  1781→    summary_text.append("Mode: ", style="bold")
  1782→    summary_text.append("Enterprise Inline\n")
  1783→    summary_text.append("Candidates: ", style="bold")
  1784→    summary_text.append(f"{len(candidates)} found, {results['skipped']} already messaged\n")
  1785→    summary_text.append("Will send to: ", style="bold")
  1786→    summary_text.append(f"{len(send_list)} candidates\n\n")
  1787→    summary_text.append("Message Preview ", style="bold cyan")
  1788→    summary_text.append(f"(first candidate):\n", style="dim")
  1789→    summary_text.append("To: ", style="bold cyan")
  1790→    recipient = first["name"]
  1791→    if first.get("title") and first.get("company"):
  1792→        recipient += f" ({first['title']} @ {first['company']})"
  1793→    summary_text.append(f"{recipient}\n\n")
  1794→    summary_text.append(first_content)
  1795→
  1796→    console.print(Panel(
  1797→        summary_text,
  1798→        title="Enterprise Pipeline Summary",
  1799→        border_style="green",
  1800→        padding=(1, 2),
  1801→    ))
  1802→
  1803→    # Confirmation
  1804→    if skip_confirmation:
  1805→        console.print(f"[green]Auto-confirmed: sending to {len(send_list)} candidates (enterprise)[/green]")
  1806→    else:
  1807→        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
  1808→        confirm = await loop.run_in_executor(
  1809→            None,
  1810→            lambda: questionary.confirm(
  1811→                f"Confirm batch send to {len(send_list)} candidates?"
  1812→            ).unsafe_ask(),
  1813→        )
  1814→
  1815→        if not confirm:
  1816→            console.print("[yellow]Batch send cancelled.[/yellow]")
  1817→            return results
  1818→
  1819→    # Send messages
  1820→    from rich.progress import Progress, BarColumn, TextColumn as ProgressTextColumn, MofNCompleteColumn
  1821→
  1822→    with Progress(
  1823→        ProgressTextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
  1824→        BarColumn(),
  1825→        MofNCompleteColumn(),
  1826→        console=console,
  1827→    ) as progress:
  1828→        task = progress.add_task("Sending messages (enterprise)...", total=len(send_list))
  1829→
  1830→        for c in send_list:
  1831→            # Rate limit check
  1832→            try:
  1833→                await async_check_rate_limit("send_message")
  1834→            except RateLimitExceededError as e:
  1835→                console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
  1836→                remaining = len(send_list) - (results["sent"] + results["failed"])
  1837→                results["rate_limited"] = remaining
  1838→                results["details"].append({
  1839→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1840→                    "name": c["name"],
  1841→                    "status": "rate_limited",
  1842→                    "reason": str(e),
  1843→                })
  1844→                break
  1845→
  1846→            # Build per-candidate variables
  1847→            variables = {
  1848→                **common_variables,
  1849→                "name": c["name"],
  1850→                "company": c["company"],
  1851→                "position": c["title"],
  1852→            }
  1853→            content = render_template(template["content"], variables)
  1854→
  1855→            # Scroll to find the candidate card (by name match)
  1856→            try:
  1857→                card_locator = page.get_by_text(c["name"], exact=False).first
  1858→                await card_locator.scroll_into_view_if_needed(timeout=5000)
  1859→                await async_random_delay("action")
  1860→            except Exception as e:
  1861→                logger.warning(f"Could not scroll to card for '{c['name']}': {e}")
  1862→
  1863→            # Send inline
  1864→            success = await send_message_enterprise_inline(page, content, config)
  1865→
  1866→            if success:
  1867→                results["sent"] += 1
  1868→                results["details"].append({
  1869→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1870→                    "name": c["name"],
  1871→                    "status": "sent",
  1872→                    "content": content,
  1873→                })
  1874→                rate_limiter.log_action("send_message", {
  1875→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1876→                    "template": template_name,
  1877→                    "mode": "enterprise",
  1878→                })
  1879→                _log_message(db_manager, c["maimai_id"], template_name, content, "sent")
  1880→            else:
  1881→                results["failed"] += 1
  1882→                logger.warning(
  1883→                    f"Enterprise inline send failed for {c['name']} ({c['maimai_id']}): "
  1884→                    f"send_message_enterprise_inline returned False"
  1885→                )
  1886→                results["details"].append({
  1887→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1888→                    "name": c["name"],
  1889→                    "status": "failed",
  1890→                    "reason": "inline send returned False",
  1891→                    "content": content,
  1892→                })
  1893→                _log_message(db_manager, c["maimai_id"], template_name, content, "failed")
  1894→
  1895→            progress.advance(task)
  1896→            await async_random_delay("message")
  1897→
  1898→    return results
  1899→

683→async def send_message_batch(
   684→    candidates: List[dict],
   685→    template_name: str,
   686→    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
   687→    browser: BrowserManager,
   688→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
   689→    interactive: bool = False,
   690→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   691→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   692→    """
   693→    Batch send messages to a list of candidates.
   694→
   695→    Flow:
   696→    1. Filter already-messaged candidates
   697→    2. Auto-fill name/company/position variables per candidate
   698→    3. Render message template
   699→    4. Show candidate list + first message preview
   700→    5. Single confirmation
   701→    6. Send one by one, checking rate limit each time
   702→    7. Skip failures and record
   703→    8. Return result stats
   704→
   705→    Args:
   706→        candidates: List of dicts with maimai_id, name, company, title
   707→        template_name: Name of message template
   708→        common_variables: Shared variables (recruiter_name, target_position, etc.)
   709→        browser: Browser manager instance
   710→        skip_messaged: Skip candidates already messaged (default True)
   711→        interactive: Enable interactive selector discovery on first failure
   712→
   713→    Returns:
   714→        Dict with total, sent, failed, skipped, rate_limited, details
   715→    """
   716→    config = get_config()
   717→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   718→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   719→
   720→    # Get template
   721→    try:
   722→        template = config.get_template(template_name)
   723→    except Exception:
   724→        raise ValidationError(f"Template not found: {template_name}")
   725→
   726→    # Create shared discovered selectors for interactive mode
   727→    if interactive:
   728→        from src.modules.selector_discovery import DiscoveredSelectors
   729→        discovered_selectors = DiscoveredSelectors()
   730→    else:
   731→        discovered_selectors = None
   732→
   733→    results = {
   734→        'total': len(candidates),
   735→        'sent': 0,
   736→        'failed': 0,
   737→        'skipped': 0,
   738→        'rate_limited': 0,
   739→        'details': [],
   740→    }
   741→
   742→    # Filter already-messaged candidates
   743→    send_list = []
   744→    if skip_messaged:
   745→        # Batch pre-fetch all sent message IDs to avoid N+1 queries
   746→        all_ids = [c['maimai_id'] for c in candidates]
   747→        messaged_ids = set()
   748→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   749→            rows = session.query(Message.candidate_maimai_id).filter(
   750→                Message.candidate_maimai_id.in_(all_ids),
   751→                Message.status == 'sent'
   752→            ).distinct().all()
   753→            messaged_ids = {row[0] for row in rows}
   754→
   755→        for c in candidates:
   756→            if c['maimai_id'] in messaged_ids:
   757→                results['skipped'] += 1
   758→                results['details'].append({
   759→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   760→                    'name': c['name'],
   761→                    'status': 'skipped',
   762→                    'reason': 'already messaged',
   763→                })
   764→            else:
   765→                send_list.append(c)
   766→    else:
   767→        send_list = list(candidates)
   768→
   769→    if not send_list:
   770→        if results["skipped"] > 0:
   771→            console.print(f"[yellow]All {results['skipped']} candidates already messaged. Skipping send.[/yellow]")
   772→        else:
   773→            console.print("[yellow]No candidates to send messages to.[/yellow]")
   774→        return results
   775→
   776→    # Pre-validate all candidates can render template (防止发送到一半才发现变量缺失)
   777→    console.print("[dim]Validating template for all candidates...[/dim]")
   778→    for c in send_list:
   779→        test_vars = {
   780→            **common_variables,
   781→            'name': c.get('name', ''),
   782→            'company': c.get('company', ''),
   783→            'position': c.get('title', ''),
   784→        }
   785→        is_valid, missing = validate_template_rendering(template['content'], test_vars, c)
   786→        if not is_valid:
   787→            raise ValidationError(
   788→                f"候选人 {c.get('name', 'Unknown')} 的模板变量缺失或为空:\n"
   789→                f"  缺失: {', '.join(missing)}\n"
   790→                f"  候选人信息: {c}\n"
   791→                f"  请检查模板或补充变量"
   792→            )
   793→
   794→    # Build preview for first candidate
   795→    first = send_list[0]
   796→    first_vars = {**common_variables, 'name': first['name'], 'company': first['company'], 'position': first['title']}
   797→    first_content = render_template(template['content'], first_vars, validate=False)  # Already validated above
   798→
   799→    # Display summary panel
   800→    summary_text = Text()
   801→    summary_text.append("Template: ", style="bold")
   802→    summary_text.append(f"{template_name}\n")
   803→    summary_text.append("Candidates: ", style="bold")
   804→    summary_text.append(f"{len(candidates)} found, {results['skipped']} already messaged\n")
   805→    summary_text.append("Will send to: ", style="bold")
   806→    summary_text.append(f"{len(send_list)} candidates\n\n")
   807→    summary_text.append("Message Preview ", style="bold cyan")
   808→    summary_text.append(f"(first candidate):\n", style="dim")
   809→    summary_text.append("To: ", style="bold cyan")
   810→    recipient = first['name']
   811→    if first.get('title') and first.get('company'):
   812→        recipient += f" ({first['title']} @ {first['company']})"
   813→    summary_text.append(f"{recipient}\n\n")
   814→    summary_text.append(first_content)
   815→
   816→    console.print(Panel(
   817→        summary_text,
   818→        title="Pipeline Summary",
   819→        border_style="green",
   820→        padding=(1, 2)
   821→    ))
   822→
   823→    # Single confirmation (skip if --yes flag or non-interactive environment)
   824→    if skip_confirmation:
   825→        console.print(f"[green]Auto-confirmed: sending to {len(send_list)} candidates[/green]")
   826→    else:
   827→        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
   828→        confirm = await loop.run_in_executor(
   829→            None,
   830→            lambda: questionary.confirm(
   831→                f"Confirm batch send to {len(send_list)} candidates?"
   832→            ).unsafe_ask()
   833→        )
   834→
   835→        if not confirm:
   836→            console.print("[yellow]Batch send cancelled.[/yellow]")
   837→            return results
   838→
   839→    # Send messages one by one
   840→    from rich.progress import Progress, BarColumn, TextColumn as ProgressTextColumn, MofNCompleteColumn
   841→
   842→    with Progress(
   843→        ProgressTextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
   844→        BarColumn(),
   845→        MofNCompleteColumn(),
   846→        console=console
   847→    ) as progress:
   848→        task = progress.add_task("Sending messages...", total=len(send_list))
   849→
   850→        max_retries = 2
   851→        rate_limit_hit = False
   852→
   853→        for c in send_list:
   854→            if rate_limit_hit:
   855→                break
   856→
   857→            # Check rate limit (waits through min-interval)
   858→            try:
   859→                await async_check_rate_limit('send_message')
   860→            except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   861→                console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
   862→                remaining = len(send_list) - (results['sent'] + results['failed'])
   863→                results['rate_limited'] = remaining
   864→                results['details'].append({
   865→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   866→                    'name': c['name'],
   867→                    'status': 'rate_limited',
   868→                    'reason': str(e),
   869→                })
   870→                break
   871→
   872→            # Build per-candidate variables
   873→            variables = {
   874→                **common_variables,
   875→                'name': c['name'],
   876→                'company': c['company'],
   877→                'position': c['title'],
   878→            }
   879→            try:
   880→                content = render_template(template['content'], variables, validate=False)  # Already validated in batch
   881→            except ValidationError as e:
   882→                # Should not happen if pre-validation passed, but handle gracefully
   883→                logger.error(f"Template rendering failed for {c['name']}: {e}")
   884→                results['failed'] += 1
   885→                results['details'].append({
   886→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   887→                    'name': c['name'],
   888→                    'status': 'failed',
   889→                    'reason': f"模板渲染失败: {e}",
   890→                })
   891→                continue
   892→
   893→            # Retry loop for transient failures
   894→            sent = False
   895→            last_error = None
   896→            per_candidate_timeout = 90  # seconds per candidate
   897→            for attempt in range(1 + max_retries):
   898→                try:
   899→                    success = await asyncio.wait_for(
   900→                        send_message(
   901→                            maimai_id=c['maimai_id'],
   902→                            template_name=template_name,
   903→                            variables=variables,
   904→                            browser=browser,
   905→                            skip_confirmation=True,
   906→                            interactive=interactive,
   907→                            discovered_selectors=discovered_selectors,
   908→                            _skip_rate_check=True,
   909→                            profile_url=c.get('profile_url'),
   910→                        ),
   911→                        timeout=per_candidate_timeout,
   912→                    )
   913→
   914→                    if success:
   915→                        results['sent'] += 1
   916→                        results['details'].append({
   917→                            'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   918→                            'name': c['name'],
   919→                            'status': 'sent',
   920→                            'attempts': attempt + 1,
   921→                            'content': content,
   922→                        })
   923→                        sent = True
   924→                        break
   925→                    else:
   926→                        last_error = "send returned False"
   927→
   928→                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   929→                    last_error = f"Timed out after {per_candidate_timeout}s"
   930→                    console.print(f"[yellow]Timeout for {c['name']} ({c['maimai_id']}), skipping.[/yellow]")
   931→                    break
   932→                except QuotaExhaustedError as e:
   933→                    # Quota is a global resource — abort the entire batch
   934→                    remaining = len(send_list) - (results['sent'] + results['failed'])
   935→                    results['failed'] += 1
   936→                    results['details'].append({
   937→                        'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   938→                        'name': c['name'],
   939→                        'status': 'quota_exhausted',
   940→                        'reason': str(e),
   941→                    })
   942→                    console.print(f"\n[red]联系券已用尽，终止批量发送 (剩余 {remaining - 1} 人未发送)[/red]")
   943→                    rate_limit_hit = True  # reuse flag to skip remaining candidates
   944→                    break
   945→                except PermanentFailureError as e:
   946→                    last_error = str(e)
   947→                    console.print(f"[yellow]{e}. Skipping.[/yellow]")
   948→                    break
   949→                except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   950→                    remaining = len(send_list) - (results['sent'] + results['failed'])
   951→                    results['rate_limited'] = remaining
   952→                    results['details'].append({
   953→                        'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   954→                        'name': c['name'],
   955→                        'status': 'rate_limited',
   956→                        'reason': str(e),
   957→                    })
   958→                    console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
   959→                    rate_limit_hit = True
   960→                    break
   961→                except Exception as e:
   962→                    last_error = str(e)
   963→
   964→                # Retry with exponential backoff if not last attempt
   965→                if attempt < max_retries and not rate_limit_hit:
   966→                    from src.utils.delays import get_retry_delay
   967→                    delay = get_retry_delay(attempt, base_delay=3.0, max_delay=30.0)
   968→                    logger.info(f"Retry {attempt + 1}/{max_retries} for {c['name']} in {delay:.1f}s")
   969→                    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   970→
   971→            if not sent and not rate_limit_hit:
   972→                actual_attempts = attempt + 1
   973→                results['failed'] += 1
   974→                results['details'].append({
   975→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   976→                    'name': c['name'],
   977→                    'status': 'failed',
   978→                    'reason': last_error,
   979→                    'attempts': actual_attempts,
   980→                    'content': content,
   981→                })
   982→                logger.error(f"Failed to send message to {c['name']} after {actual_attempts} attempt(s): {last_error}")
   983→
   984→            progress.advance(task)
   985→
   986→            # Emit progress for web service
   987→            emit_progress("send_progress",
   988→                          sent=results['sent'], failed=results['failed'],
   989→                          total=len(send_list), current=c['name'])
   990→
   991→            # Human-like delay between messages
   992→            await async_random_delay('message')
   993→
   994→    return results

1550→def _log_message(
  1551→    db_manager,
  1552→    maimai_id: str,
  1553→    template_name: str,
  1554→    content: str,
  1555→    status: str
  1556→) -> None:
  1557→    """Log message to database. Creates a minimal Candidate if needed for FK."""
  1558→    try:
  1559→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
  1560→            # Ensure candidate exists for FK constraint
  1561→            existing = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(maimai_id=maimai_id).first()
  1562→            if not existing:
  1563→                session.add(Candidate(maimai_id=maimai_id))
  1564→                session.flush()
  1565→
  1566→            message = Message(
  1567→                candidate_maimai_id=maimai_id,
  1568→                template_name=template_name,
  1569→                content=content,
  1570→                status=status,
  1571→                sent_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) if status == 'sent' else None
  1572→            )

1615→async def send_message_enterprise_inline(
  1616→    page: Page,
  1617→    content: str,
  1618→    config=None,
  1619→) -> bool:
  1620→    """Send a message inline on the enterprise recruitment page.
  1621→
  1622→    This operates on the current visible candidate card — clicks "立即沟通",
  1623→    types the message in the textarea, clicks "发送后留在此页", and closes
  1624→    the popup.
  1625→
  1626→    Args:
  1627→        page: Playwright page on the enterprise results page
  1628→        content: Message content to send
  1629→        config: Config instance (loaded if None)
  1630→
  1631→    Returns:
  1632→        True if message was sent successfully
  1633→    """
  1634→    if config is None:
  1635→        config = get_config()
  1636→
  1637→    try:
  1638→        # Click 立即沟通
  1639→        chat_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.chat_now_button")
  1640→        chat_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(chat_sel, timeout=5000)
  1641→        if not chat_btn:
  1642→            logger.warning("Enterprise: 立即沟通 button not found")
  1643→            return False
  1644→
  1645→        await human_click(page, chat_sel)
  1646→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1647→
  1648→        # Wait for message textarea
  1649→        textarea_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.message_textarea")
  1650→        textarea = await page.wait_for_selector(textarea_sel, timeout=5000)
  1651→        if not textarea:
  1652→            logger.warning("Enterprise: message textarea not found")
  1653→            return False
  1654→
  1655→        # Clear any default text and type message
  1656→        await textarea.click()
  1657→        select_all_key = "Meta+a" if sys.platform == "darwin" else "Control+a"
  1658→        await page.keyboard.press(select_all_key)
  1659→        await page.keyboard.press("Backspace")
  1660→        await human_type(page, textarea_sel, content)
  1661→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1662→
  1663→        # Click 发送后留在此页
  1664→        send_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.send_stay_button")
  1665→        send_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(send_sel, timeout=5000)
  1666→        if not send_btn:
  1667→            logger.warning("Enterprise: send button not found")
  1668→            return False
  1669→
  1670→        await human_click(page, send_sel)
  1671→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1672→
  1673→        # Close popup/modal
  1674→        await _close_enterprise_popup(page)
  1675→
  1676→        # Verify message was actually sent
  1677→        verified = await _verify_message_sent(page, content)
  1678→        if not verified:
  1679→            logger.warning("Enterprise: message send could not be verified")
  1680→            return False
  1681→
  1682→        logger.info("Enterprise: message sent and verified inline")
  1683→        return True
  1684→
  1685→    except Exception as e:
  1686→        logger.error(f"Enterprise inline send failed: {e}")
  1687→        return False
  1688→
